Dear Mr.

Fair Political Practices Commission 
llOO K Street 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Sirs: 
U'llo. 
.4LLEN Pp.RK'NAY 
n 
\) 
GO\( 
\ \ l2 ~ 
riOUSTON. TEXAS nco 1 
71 
June 23, 1978 
We would appreciate some information on filing requirements 
for corporation-sponsored political action committees. 
Our committee 
receives all of its money from employees of our company and affiliated 
companies. 
The corporation merely administers and solicits employee 
contributions to the fund. 
It is structured like the federal political 
action committees, but limits itself to California state elections. 
Is our committee required to file a "major donor's statement" 
if it makes expenditures greater than $5,000 in a calendar year? 
Or, 
does the committee merely report as a "recipient committee", (which we 
plan to do)? 
In other words, regarding the types of filings required, 
are the categories in § 820l3(a), (b), and (c) mutually exclusive? If 
our committee reports in category (a), must it also report as in category 
(c)? 
To use the language of your 1978 Information Manual, page 3, can a 
corporate political action committee be simultaneously a "recipient 
committee" and a "major donor committee"? 
We would appreciate your answer at your earliest convenience. 
Thank you very much. 
Sincerely, 
kIm 


1 
770 1 
7, 1 7

sections.question: We would appreciate some information on filing requirements for corporation-sponsored political action committees. Our committee receives all of its money from employees of our company and affiliated companies. The corporation merely administers and solicits employee contributions to the fund. It is structured like the federal political action committees, but limits itself to California state elections. Is our committee required to file a "major donor's statement" if it makes expenditures greater than $5,000 in a calendar year? Or, does the committee merely report as a "recipient committee", (which we plan to do)? In other words, regarding the types of filings required, are the categories in § 82013(a), (b), and (c) mutually exclusive? If our committee reports in category (a), must it also report as in category (c)? To use the language of your 1978 Information Manual, page 3, can a corporate political action committee be simultaneously a "recipient committee" and a "major donor committee"?
sections.question_synthetic: The inquirer asks whether a corporation-sponsored political action committee that receives contributions from employees must file as a 'major donor' or 'recipient committee' under California law, and whether these filing categories are mutually exclusive.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated June 23, 1978 to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document ID suggests it was filed with FPPC on July 17, 1978. No conclusion or FPPC response is included in this document. The document appears to be the initial inquiry only.

embedding.summary: An incoming request letter from a Texas-based company's political action committee seeking clarification on California FPPC filing requirements for corporation-sponsored PACs, specifically regarding whether they must file as major donors or recipient committees.
